description: Cambridge Analytica harvested data from 87M Facebook users. Tests 'too big to fail' platform dynamics — trust eroded permanently but user base barely declined due to network lock-in.

key_events.description: NYT/Guardian expose. #DeleteFacebook trends globally. Stock drops 7%, losing $50B market cap. Zuckerberg silent 5 days.
Zuckerberg CNN interview and newspaper apology ads. Scope revised to 87M users. FTC confirms investigation.
Zuckerberg Congressional testimony — seen as a 'win' due to senators' poor tech literacy. Stock rises 4.5%.
NYT reports data-sharing partnerships with 60+ device makers including Huawei. Reignites concerns.
Q2 earnings: stock drops 19% in single day — $119B market cap loss, largest in US history at the time.
NYT 'Delay, Deny, Deflect' investigation reveals aggressive lobbying and opposition research against critics.

calibration_notes: Tests unique 'too big to fail' platform dynamics. User base barely declined despite massive trust erosion (network lock-in). Congressional testimony can actually improve stock if executives perform well (Round 4 reversal). Crisis re-escalation: sentiment stabilizes then gets hit by new revelations (Rounds 6, 8, 9). Pew: trust dropped from 79% to 53% for data handling. $5B FTC fine. Eventually rebranded to Meta (Oct 2021).
